        <tool name="past_prs_search" label="Past PR Search" use_when="Find prior accepted or rejected PR evidence for a file, subsystem, tactic, or review risk." provider="past_prs" type="history" />
        <tool name="path_facts_resolve" label="Path Facts Resolve" use_when="Resolve accepted path-scoped facts and directory-slice hints for a source path." provider="path_facts" type="path_context" />
        <tool name="mismatch_db_search" label="Mismatch DB Search" use_when="Search known mismatch symptoms and source-shape tactics after a concrete diff symptom." provider="mismatch_db" type="mismatch_research" />
        <tool name="checkdiff_run" label="Checkdiff Run" use_when="Run focused checkdiff/objdiff output for one function instead of raw asm-differ shell commands." provider="checkdiff" type="verification" />
        <tool name="checkdiff_summary" label="Checkdiff Summary" use_when="Run PASS/FAIL summaries for a target and affected neighbors instead of raw asm-differ shell commands." provider="checkdiff" type="verification" />
        <tool name="direct_compile_tu" label="Direct Compile TU" use_when="Compile one function's translation unit to separate build failure from objdiff mismatch." provider="checkdiff" type="verification" />

<decomp_standards>
    <instruction>
        Use each standard as an example-backed source-quality pattern: read the description,
        compare the bad/preferred code pair, and apply the same transformation only when local evidence supports it.
    </instruction>
    <standard id="natural-loops">
        <description>
            - Repeated blocks that vary by an index, pointer, flag slot, or stride are often original loops.
            - Try natural for/while/do-while forms, including exact int counters when MWCC loop shape matters, before keeping copied generated blocks.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="natural-loop-pointer-stride" severity="pre_ship_review">
            <bad_code>if (count != 0) {
    cur = groups;
    do {
        HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress(cur);
        cur++;
        count--;
    } while (count != 0);
}</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>for (i = 0; i < count; i++) {
    HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress(&groups[i]);
}
/* Keep the source loop zero-or-more and indexable when the array count is known. */</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - A guarded pointer-walk can be decompiler residue for an ordinary counted loop.
                - The counted loop exposes the authored array shape and avoids preserving control flow solely because it currently matches.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - m2c_goto_label
            - m2c_residue_names
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="infer-authored-source-style">
        <description>
            - Matching should reconstruct likely original authored source, not arbitrary C that moves a score.
            - Use matched siblings, nearby files, headers, macros, naming habits, control-flow idioms, and review evidence to infer the original developers' local standards before preserving generated, permuter-shaped, or data-driven source.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="authored-style-local-inline-count" severity="pre_ship_review">
            <bad_code>PAD_STACK(8);
count = 0;
if (mnEvent_8024D1E4(0) != 0) count++;
if (mnEvent_8024D1E4(1) != 0) count++;
if (mnEvent_8024D1E4(2) != 0) count++;</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>static inline s32 mnEvent_CountUnlocked(void)
{
    s32 i;
    s32 count = 0;

    for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
        if (mnEvent_8024D1E4(i) != 0) {
            count++;
        }
    }
    return count;
}

count = mnEvent_CountUnlocked();</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Local sibling helpers are stronger source-shape evidence than stack padding plus repeated generated calls.
                - Restore the authored helper boundary when nearby code shows the project used that boundary.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
    </standard>
    <standard id="typed-fields-over-pointer-math">
        <description>
            - When a type can describe an access, prefer real fields, correct FighterVars/GroundVars/ItemVars union arms, accessors, or temporary internal structs before M2C_FIELD and raw pointer arithmetic.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="typed-item-vars-owning-base" qa_rule_id="pointer_offset_arithmetic" severity="warning">
            <bad_code>Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj);
value = *(s32*) ((u8*) ip + 0xE38);</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>typedef struct itArwingLaser_ItemVars {
    char x0_pad[0x64];
    s32 xE38;
} itArwingLaser_ItemVars;

value = ip->xDD4_itemVar.arwinglaser.xE38;</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Recover item-var fields from the owning Item.xDD4 base, not from the first offset that happens to be used.
                - The typed union arm documents ownership and keeps later offsets from drifting.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - stage_ground_var_owner
            - m2c_field_use
            - type_erasing_cast
            - pointer_offset_arithmetic
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="header-inlines">
        <description>
            - Expanded header asserts, especially from jobj.h, should be mapped back to the existing inline/helper when line numbers and local evidence identify the source-level operation.
            - Local helper boundaries should also be restored when duplicated expanded bodies or sibling functions show an authored helper shape.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="header-inline-jobj-helper" qa_rule_id="copied_jobj_inline" severity="error">
            <bad_code>static inline void LocalSetMtxDirty(HSD_JObj* jobj)
{
    if (jobj == NULL) {
        __assert("jobj.h", 0x2F4, "jobj");
    }
    jobj->flags |= JOBJ_MTX_DIRTY;
}</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty(jobj);
/* Or use the shared WithMtxDirty variant when that is the evidenced helper. */</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Local fake helpers that mirror jobj.h asserts and writes should be compared against the owning header helper.
                - The fix path is to recover the canonical helper rather than copy the inline body into each TU.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - copied_jobj_inline
            - unrolled_assert
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="assert-report-macros">
        <description>
            - Raw assert/report expansions should usually become HSD_ASSERT, HSD_ASSERTMSG, HSD_ASSERTREPORT, OSReport, or OSPanic forms when the file, line, message, and side effects match local project idioms.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="assert-report-direct-asserts" qa_rule_id="unrolled_assert" severity="error">
            <bad_code>if (obj == NULL) {
    __assert(__FILE__, 0x88, "obj");
}</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>HSD_ASSERT(0x88, obj);</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - A direct assert block should become the project assert macro when file, line, condition, and side effects match.
                - Leave expanded forms only when the macro form would change object bytes or behavior.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - unrolled_assert
            - fake_assert_macro
            - assert_idiom_downgrade
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="literals-and-data-ownership">
        <description>
            - Do not promote strings, floats, or constants into named storage just because generated output exposed an address.
            - Check section ownership, symbol metadata, split boundaries, and local source patterns first.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="literal-extern-float-anchor" qa_rule_id="extern_literal_anchor" severity="error">
            <bad_code>extern const f32 lbl_804DA60C;

speed = lbl_804DA60C;

const f32 lbl_804DA60C = 1.0F;</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>speed = 1.0F;
/* Keep named data only when symbol metadata and section ownership prove it. */</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Introducing an extern, use, and definition together creates a data-order anchor instead of recovering source ownership.
                - Ordinary numeric constants should stay inline unless real data ownership is proven.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - extern_literal_anchor
            - new_data_anchor
            - self_tu_extern
            - numeric_literal_to_symbol
            - address_named_static_data
            - banned_pattern:*
            - resubmission_tombstone
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="no-string-literal-symbol-regression">
        <description>
            - String literals used as asset names, resource labels, assert/report text, and table labels should stay inline for code-matching changes.
            - Replacing a literal with a generated or global data symbol is data work and a regression unless evidence and change scope justify it.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="string-asset-label-inline" qa_rule_id="string_literal_to_symbol" severity="error">
            <bad_code>extern char mnNameNew_803EE38C[];

HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress(archive, mnNameNew_803EE38C);</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress(archive, "MenMainBack_Top_joint");
/* Keep ordinary asset labels inline unless data ownership is the explicit scope. */</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Asset and resource labels should not become address-style globals just to chase relocation or data layout.
                - This replacement makes a source literal look like a data-symbol ownership claim.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - extern_literal_anchor
            - string_literal_to_symbol
            - packed_string_blob
            - banned_pattern:*
            - resubmission_tombstone
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="canonical-control-flow-and-macros">
        <description>
            - Generated branch ladders, gotos, repeated select-like assignments, and raw absolute/min/max expressions should be checked against normal source forms such as switch, ternary, ABS, MIN, MAX, and CLAMP.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="canonical-switch-dispatch" qa_rule_id="m2c_goto_label" severity="warning">
            <bad_code>if (kind == 0) goto lbl_idle;
if (kind == 1) goto lbl_walk;
if (kind == 2) goto lbl_jump;
goto lbl_default;</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>switch (kind) {
case 0: return idle();
case 1: return walk();
case 2: return jump();
default: return fallback();
}</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Generated-looking dispatch chains should be checked against grouped switch forms.
                - This shape is goto residue; grouped switch dispatch is clearer and usually closer to authored source.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - m2c_goto_label
            - define_alias
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="matching-tactics-need-evidence">
        <description>
            - PAD_STACK, declaration order changes, widened local lifetimes, dummy locals, volatile locals, manual inline expansion, direct global access, and temporary padded structs need explicit build/objdiff/regression evidence before they remain in reviewable source.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="matching-pad-stack-sp-pad" severity="warning">
            <bad_code>s32 sp[4];
UNUSED u8 sp_pad[8];
s32 i;

UseStack(sp);</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>s32 sp[4];
s32 i;

PAD_STACK(8);

UseStack(sp);
/* Keep stack padding narrow and backed by objdiff evidence. */</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Manual UNUSED padding arrays are dummy locals posing as source structure.
                - When intentional padding is required, use PAD_STACK(N) and keep the tactic evidence-bound.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - function_extern_visibility
            - same_tu_function_extern
            - volatile_local_tactic
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="avoid-pragmas-register-asm">
        <description>
            - Pragmas, register keywords, and inline assembly are exceptions, not ordinary decomp style.
            - Scope unavoidable exceptions tightly and prove they do not harm neighbors.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="avoid-inline-asm-thpinit" qa_rule_id="inline_asm" severity="error">
            <bad_code>asm BOOL THPInit(void)
{
    nofralloc
    mflr r0
    ...
}</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>BOOL THPInit(void)
{
    /* Recover the C body and keep raw asm out of normal source. */
    ...
}</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Raw inline assembly is not acceptable normal decomp source when a C implementation can be recovered.
                - Recover the C implementation instead of retaining an asm body for normal source.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - register_keyword
            - inline_asm
            - novel_pragma
            - codegen_pragma
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="conservative-naming">
        <description>
            - Names should improve reviewability without inventing meaning.
            - Use semantic names when supported, keep fn_/lbl_/address-style names when semantics are not proven, and clean temp_rXX names once a local role is clear.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="conservative-address-function-name" severity="pre_ship_review">
            <bad_code>s32 mnName_GetUnlockedColumnCount(void)
{
    return mnName_802388D4();
}
/* Semantic name is guessed from behavior only. */</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>s32 mnName_802388D4(void)
{
    return count;
}
/* Keep the address-style name until source or review evidence proves the role. */</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Do not invent semantic names just because a function body became easier to understand.
                - Preserve address-style names when stronger naming evidence is not present.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - m2c_residue_names
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="no-define-alias-global-renames">
        <description>
            - A global, extern, or data symbol should have one canonical name backed by source, symbol, map, or review evidence.
            - Do not hide speculative or convenience renames behind identifier-to-identifier defines, and do not keep duplicate address-commented extern declarations for the same data address.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="define-alias-central-symbol-rename" qa_rule_id="define_alias" severity="error">
            <bad_code>#define HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize AXDriver_8038E034

size = HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize();</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>/* config/GALE01/symbols.txt */
/* Rename .text:0x8038E034 from AXDriver_8038E034 to HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize. */
size = HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize();</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - A canonical rename belongs in central symbol metadata or direct references, not in a source alias.
                - Perform the evidenced rename once instead of masking duplicate names with a define.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - define_alias
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="truthful-headers-and-includes">
        <description>
            - When a body proves a signature, update the owning header and remove UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
            - Use established include style and run require-protos checks when prototype or include surfaces change.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="headers-include-proto-shim" severity="pre_ship_review">
            <bad_code>#define __THPReadFrameHeader __THPReadFrameHeader_proto
#define THPDec_80330158 THPDec_80330158_proto
#include <dolphin/thp/thp.h>
#undef THPDec_80330158
#undef __THPReadFrameHeader

static u8 __THPReadFrameHeader(THPFileInfo* info);</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>#include <dolphin/thp/thp.h>

/* dolphin/thp/thp.h owns the recovered signatures. */
static u8 __THPReadFrameHeader(THPFileInfo*);
u8 THPDec_80330158(THPFileInfo* info);</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Macro-renaming declarations around an include hides a prototype conflict instead of fixing the owning header.
                - Repair the owning header signature and include it normally instead of using macro/prototype shims around the include.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - function_extern_visibility
            - same_tu_function_extern
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
</decomp_standards>

<decomp_standards>
    <instruction>
        Use each standard as an example-backed source-quality pattern: read the description,
        compare the bad/preferred code pair, and apply the same transformation only when local evidence supports it.
    </instruction>
    <standard id="natural-loops">
        <description>
            - Repeated blocks that vary by an index, pointer, flag slot, or stride are often original loops.
            - Try natural for/while/do-while forms, including exact int counters when MWCC loop shape matters, before keeping copied generated blocks.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="natural-loop-pointer-stride" severity="pre_ship_review">
            <bad_code>if (count != 0) {
    cur = groups;
    do {
        HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress(cur);
        cur++;
        count--;
    } while (count != 0);
}</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>for (i = 0; i < count; i++) {
    HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress(&groups[i]);
}
/* Keep the source loop zero-or-more and indexable when the array count is known. */</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - A guarded pointer-walk can be decompiler residue for an ordinary counted loop.
                - The counted loop exposes the authored array shape and avoids preserving control flow solely because it currently matches.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - m2c_goto_label
            - m2c_residue_names
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="infer-authored-source-style">
        <description>
            - Matching should reconstruct likely original authored source, not arbitrary C that moves a score.
            - Use matched siblings, nearby files, headers, macros, naming habits, control-flow idioms, and review evidence to infer the original developers' local standards before preserving generated, permuter-shaped, or data-driven source.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="authored-style-local-inline-count" severity="pre_ship_review">
            <bad_code>PAD_STACK(8);
count = 0;
if (mnEvent_8024D1E4(0) != 0) count++;
if (mnEvent_8024D1E4(1) != 0) count++;
if (mnEvent_8024D1E4(2) != 0) count++;</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>static inline s32 mnEvent_CountUnlocked(void)
{
    s32 i;
    s32 count = 0;

    for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
        if (mnEvent_8024D1E4(i) != 0) {
            count++;
        }
    }
    return count;
}

count = mnEvent_CountUnlocked();</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Local sibling helpers are stronger source-shape evidence than stack padding plus repeated generated calls.
                - Restore the authored helper boundary when nearby code shows the project used that boundary.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
    </standard>
    <standard id="typed-fields-over-pointer-math">
        <description>
            - When a type can describe an access, prefer real fields, correct FighterVars/GroundVars/ItemVars union arms, accessors, or temporary internal structs before M2C_FIELD and raw pointer arithmetic.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="typed-item-vars-owning-base" qa_rule_id="pointer_offset_arithmetic" severity="warning">
            <bad_code>Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj);
value = *(s32*) ((u8*) ip + 0xE38);</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>typedef struct itArwingLaser_ItemVars {
    char x0_pad[0x64];
    s32 xE38;
} itArwingLaser_ItemVars;

value = ip->xDD4_itemVar.arwinglaser.xE38;</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Recover item-var fields from the owning Item.xDD4 base, not from the first offset that happens to be used.
                - The typed union arm documents ownership and keeps later offsets from drifting.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - stage_ground_var_owner
            - m2c_field_use
            - type_erasing_cast
            - pointer_offset_arithmetic
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="header-inlines">
        <description>
            - Expanded header asserts, especially from jobj.h, should be mapped back to the existing inline/helper when line numbers and local evidence identify the source-level operation.
            - Local helper boundaries should also be restored when duplicated expanded bodies or sibling functions show an authored helper shape.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="header-inline-jobj-helper" qa_rule_id="copied_jobj_inline" severity="error">
            <bad_code>static inline void LocalSetMtxDirty(HSD_JObj* jobj)
{
    if (jobj == NULL) {
        __assert("jobj.h", 0x2F4, "jobj");
    }
    jobj->flags |= JOBJ_MTX_DIRTY;
}</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty(jobj);
/* Or use the shared WithMtxDirty variant when that is the evidenced helper. */</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Local fake helpers that mirror jobj.h asserts and writes should be compared against the owning header helper.
                - The fix path is to recover the canonical helper rather than copy the inline body into each TU.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - copied_jobj_inline
            - unrolled_assert
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="assert-report-macros">
        <description>
            - Raw assert/report expansions should usually become HSD_ASSERT, HSD_ASSERTMSG, HSD_ASSERTREPORT, OSReport, or OSPanic forms when the file, line, message, and side effects match local project idioms.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="assert-report-direct-asserts" qa_rule_id="unrolled_assert" severity="error">
            <bad_code>if (obj == NULL) {
    __assert(__FILE__, 0x88, "obj");
}</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>HSD_ASSERT(0x88, obj);</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - A direct assert block should become the project assert macro when file, line, condition, and side effects match.
                - Leave expanded forms only when the macro form would change object bytes or behavior.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>

/* Keep named data only when symbol metadata and section ownership prove it. */</preferred_code>

        </description>
        <canonical_example id="string-asset-label-inline" qa_rule_id="string_literal_to_symbol" severity="error">
            <bad_code>extern char mnNameNew_803EE38C[];

HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress(archive, mnNameNew_803EE38C);</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress(archive, "MenMainBack_Top_joint");
/* Keep ordinary asset labels inline unless data ownership is the explicit scope. */</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Asset and resource labels should not become address-style globals just to chase relocation or data layout.
                - This replacement makes a source literal look like a data-symbol ownership claim.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - extern_literal_anchor
            - string_literal_to_symbol
            - packed_string_blob
            - banned_pattern:*
            - resubmission_tombstone
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="canonical-control-flow-and-macros">
        <description>
            - Generated branch ladders, gotos, repeated select-like assignments, and raw absolute/min/max expressions should be checked against normal source forms such as switch, ternary, ABS, MIN, MAX, and CLAMP.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="canonical-switch-dispatch" qa_rule_id="m2c_goto_label" severity="warning">
            <bad_code>if (kind == 0) goto lbl_idle;
if (kind == 1) goto lbl_walk;
if (kind == 2) goto lbl_jump;
goto lbl_default;</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>switch (kind) {
case 0: return idle();
case 1: return walk();
case 2: return jump();
default: return fallback();
}</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Generated-looking dispatch chains should be checked against grouped switch forms.
                - This shape is goto residue; grouped switch dispatch is clearer and usually closer to authored source.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - m2c_goto_label
            - define_alias
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="matching-tactics-need-evidence">
        <description>
            - PAD_STACK, declaration order changes, widened local lifetimes, dummy locals, volatile locals, manual inline expansion, direct global access, and temporary padded structs need explicit build/objdiff/regression evidence before they remain in reviewable source.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="matching-pad-stack-sp-pad" severity="warning">
            <bad_code>s32 sp[4];
UNUSED u8 sp_pad[8];
s32 i;

UseStack(sp);</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>s32 sp[4];
s32 i;

PAD_STACK(8);

UseStack(sp);
/* Keep stack padding narrow and backed by objdiff evidence. */</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Manual UNUSED padding arrays are dummy locals posing as source structure.
                - When intentional padding is required, use PAD_STACK(N) and keep the tactic evidence-bound.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - function_extern_visibility
            - same_tu_function_extern
            - volatile_local_tactic
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="avoid-pragmas-register-asm">
        <description>
            - Pragmas, register keywords, and inline assembly are exceptions, not ordinary decomp style.
            - Scope unavoidable exceptions tightly and prove they do not harm neighbors.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="avoid-inline-asm-thpinit" qa_rule_id="inline_asm" severity="error">
            <bad_code>asm BOOL THPInit(void)
{
    nofralloc
    mflr r0
    ...
}</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>BOOL THPInit(void)
{
    /* Recover the C body and keep raw asm out of normal source. */
    ...
}</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Raw inline assembly is not acceptable normal decomp source when a C implementation can be recovered.
                - Recover the C implementation instead of retaining an asm body for normal source.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - register_keyword
            - inline_asm
            - novel_pragma
            - codegen_pragma
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="conservative-naming">
        <description>
            - Names should improve reviewability without inventing meaning.
            - Use semantic names when supported, keep fn_/lbl_/address-style names when semantics are not proven, and clean temp_rXX names once a local role is clear.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="conservative-address-function-name" severity="pre_ship_review">
            <bad_code>s32 mnName_GetUnlockedColumnCount(void)
{
    return mnName_802388D4();
}
/* Semantic name is guessed from behavior only. */</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>s32 mnName_802388D4(void)
{
    return count;
}
/* Keep the address-style name until source or review evidence proves the role. */</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Do not invent semantic names just because a function body became easier to understand.
                - Preserve address-style names when stronger naming evidence is not present.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - m2c_residue_names
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="no-define-alias-global-renames">
        <description>
            - A global, extern, or data symbol should have one canonical name backed by source, symbol, map, or review evidence.
            - Do not hide speculative or convenience renames behind identifier-to-identifier defines, and do not keep duplicate address-commented extern declarations for the same data address.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="define-alias-central-symbol-rename" qa_rule_id="define_alias" severity="error">
            <bad_code>#define HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize AXDriver_8038E034

size = HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize();</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>/* config/GALE01/symbols.txt */
/* Rename .text:0x8038E034 from AXDriver_8038E034 to HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize. */
size = HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize();</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - A canonical rename belongs in central symbol metadata or direct references, not in a source alias.
                - Perform the evidenced rename once instead of masking duplicate names with a define.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - define_alias
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>
    <standard id="truthful-headers-and-includes">
        <description>
            - When a body proves a signature, update the owning header and remove UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
            - Use established include style and run require-protos checks when prototype or include surfaces change.
        </description>
        <canonical_example id="headers-include-proto-shim" severity="pre_ship_review">
            <bad_code>#define __THPReadFrameHeader __THPReadFrameHeader_proto
#define THPDec_80330158 THPDec_80330158_proto
#include <dolphin/thp/thp.h>
#undef THPDec_80330158
#undef __THPReadFrameHeader

static u8 __THPReadFrameHeader(THPFileInfo* info);</bad_code>
            <preferred_code>#include <dolphin/thp/thp.h>

/* dolphin/thp/thp.h owns the recovered signatures. */
static u8 __THPReadFrameHeader(THPFileInfo*);
u8 THPDec_80330158(THPFileInfo* info);</preferred_code>
            <why>
                - Macro-renaming declarations around an include hides a prototype conflict instead of fixing the owning header.
                - Repair the owning header signature and include it normally instead of using macro/prototype shims around the include.
            </why>
        </canonical_example>
        <qa_rules>
            - function_extern_visibility
            - same_tu_function_extern
        </qa_rules>
    </standard>

content.text:
ninja_required_version = 1.3

# The arguments passed to configure.py, for rerunning it.
configure_args = --require-protos --wrapper /Users/Ford/Github $
    Repos/oss/gamecube-decomp-harness/projects/melee/state/tools/wibo
python = "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.12/bin/python3"

# Variables
ldflags = -fp hardware -nodefaults -warn off -msgstyle std
mw_version = GC/1.3.2
objdiff_report_args = 

# Tooling
rule download_tool
  command = $python tools/download_tool.py $tool $out --tag $tag
  description = TOOL $out
rule decompctx
  command = $python tools/decompctx.py $in -o $out -d $out.d $includes $
      $excludes $defines
  description = CTX $in
  depfile = $out.d
  deps = gcc
build build/tools/dtk: download_tool | tools/download_tool.py
  tool = dtk
  tag = v1.8.3
build build/tools/objdiff-cli: download_tool | tools/download_tool.py
  tool = objdiff-cli
  tag = v3.6.1
build build/tools/sjiswrap.exe: download_tool | tools/download_tool.py
  tool = sjiswrap
  tag = v1.2.2
build build/compilers: download_tool | tools/download_tool.py
  tool = compilers
  tag = 20251118
build build/binutils: download_tool | tools/download_tool.py
  tool = binutils
  tag = 2.42-2

# Download all tools
build tools: phony build/tools/dtk build/tools/sjiswrap.exe $
    /Users/Ford/Github$ Repos/oss/gamecube-decomp-harness/projects/melee/state/tools/wibo $
    build/compilers build/binutils build/tools/objdiff-cli

# Link ELF file
rule link
  command = /Users/Ford/Github $
      Repos/oss/gamecube-decomp-harness/projects/melee/state/tools/wibo $
      build/compilers/$mw_version/mwldeppc.exe $ldflags -o $out @$out.rsp
  description = LINK $out
  rspfile = $out.rsp
  rspfile_content = $in_newline

# Generate DOL
rule elf2dol
  command = build/tools/dtk elf2dol $in $out
  description = DOL $out

# MWCC build
rule mwcc
  command = /Users/Ford/Github $
      Repos/oss/gamecube-decomp-harness/projects/melee/state/tools/wibo $
      build/compilers/$mw_version/mwcceppc.exe $cflags -MMD -c $in -o $
      $basedir && $python tools/transform_dep.py $basefile.d $basefile.d
  description = MWCC $out
  depfile = $basefile.d
  deps = gcc

# MWCC build (with UTF-8 to Shift JIS wrapper)
rule mwcc_sjis
  command = /Users/Ford/Github $
      Repos/oss/gamecube-decomp-harness/projects/melee/state/tools/wibo $
      build/tools/sjiswrap.exe build/compilers/$mw_version/mwcceppc.exe $
      $cflags -MMD -c $in -o $basedir && $python tools/transform_dep.py $
      $basefile.d $basefile.d
  description = MWCC $out
  depfile = $basefile.d
  deps = gcc

# MWCC build (with extab post-processing)
rule mwcc_extab
  command = /Users/Ford/Github $
      Repos/oss/gamecube-decomp-harness/projects/melee/state/tools/wibo $
      build/compilers/$mw_version/mwcceppc.exe $cflags -MMD -c $in -o $
      $basedir && build/tools/dtk extab clean --padding "$extab_padding" $
      $out $out && $python tools/transform_dep.py $basefile.d $basefile.d
  description = MWCC $out
  depfile = $basefile.d
  deps = gcc

# MWCC build (with UTF-8 to Shift JIS wrapper and extab post-processing)
rule mwcc_sjis_extab
  command = /Users/Ford/Github $
      Repos/oss/gamecube-decomp-harness/projects/melee/state/tools/wibo $
      build/tools/sjiswrap.exe build/compilers/$mw_version/mwcceppc.exe $
      $cflags -MMD -c $in -o $basedir && build/tools/dtk extab clean $
      --padding "$extab_padding" $out $out && $python $
      tools/transform_dep.py $basefile.d $basefile.d
  description = MWCC $out
  depfile = $basefile.d
  deps = gcc
# Assemble asm
rule as
  command = build/binutils/powerpc-eabi-as $asflags -o $out $in && $
      build/tools/dtk elf fixup $out $out
  description = AS $out

# Build precompiled header
rule mwcc_pch
  command = /Users/Ford/Github $
      Repos/oss/gamecube-decomp-harness/projects/melee/state/tools/wibo $
      build/compilers/$mw_version/mwcceppc.exe $cflags -MMD -c $in -o $
      $basedir -precompile $basefilestem.mch && $python $
      tools/transform_dep.py $basefile.d $basefile.d
  description = PCH $out
  depfile = $basefile.d
  deps = gcc

# Build precompiled header (with UTF-8 to Shift JIS wrapper)
rule mwcc_pch_sjis
  command = /Users/Ford/Github $
      Repos/oss/gamecube-decomp-harness/projects/melee/state/tools/wibo $
      build/tools/sjiswrap.exe build/compilers/$mw_version/mwcceppc.exe $
      $cflags -MMD -c $in -o $basedir -precompile $basefilestem.mch && $
      $python tools/transform_dep.py $basefile.d $basefile.d
  description = PCH $out
  depfile = $basefile.d
  deps = gcc

build pre-compile: phony
# Source files
# Split DOL into relocatable objects
rule split
  command = build/tools/dtk dol split $in $out_dir
  description = SPLIT $in
  depfile = $out_dir/dep
  deps = gcc
build build/GALE01/config.json: split config/GALE01/config.yml | $
    build/tools/dtk
  out_dir = build/GALE01
